CONFIDENTIAL MEDICAL REPORT

Patient ID: MED-2024-0892
Date: November 15, 2024

Chief Complaint: Persistent headache and fatigue

History of Present Illness:
The patient presents with chronic migraines that have persisted for approximately three months. Associated symptoms include photophobia, nausea, and occasional vertigo. The patient reports that acetaminophen provides minimal relief.

Medications:
- Ibuprofen 400mg PRN for pain
- Sumatriptan 50mg for acute migraine episodes
- Propranolol 80mg daily for prophylaxis

Vital Signs:
Blood Pressure: 128/82 mmHg
Heart Rate: 72 bpm
Temperature: 98.6°F
Respiratory Rate: 16 breaths/min

Laboratory Results:
Hemoglobin: 14.2 g/dL (normal range: 12-16)
White Blood Cell Count: 7,500 cells/μL
Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH): 2.8 mIU/L
Vitamin D: 22 ng/mL (slightly deficient)

Assessment:
Primary diagnosis: Migraine with aura (G43.1)
Secondary: Vitamin D deficiency

Treatment Plan:
Continue current migraine prophylaxis. Prescribe Vitamin D supplementation 2000 IU daily. Follow up in 6 weeks to assess treatment efficacy. Consider neurological consultation if symptoms persist.
